open_gaps.SEC-002: LOW (unchanged). Still no numPages>1000 cap at the viewer.js loadDocument() load boundary, and no cumulative-queued-bytes cap at the multi-file ingest boundaries (merge.js queued PDFs, img2pdf.js queued images). This tick's three new modules (nup.js TASK-353, margins.js TASK-354, booklet.js TASK-355) are NOT new ingest boundaries — each reads ONLY the ALREADY-OPEN, already-validated document via currentDoc.getData(), builds a FRESH pdf-lib doc (open doc never mutated), and uploads nothing. They are pure pdf-lib structural imposition (embedPages/embedPage + drawPage, no rasterization — low memory, safe for the 1.6 GiB box) and produce BOUNDED output: nup = ceil(numPages/n) sheets, margins = numPages pages (with a MAX_MM=100 margin cap), booklet = ceil(srcPages/4)*2 landscape faces. Same LOW self-DoS family as the other per-page consumers (uploader's own tab only, no server impact), bounded by the same single fix — the viewer-load page-count cap. No new boundary, no separate cap needed beyond that. All remaining gaps are client-side-only DoS on the 1.6 GiB box; no server-side risk. Single right developer fix remains: page-count cap once at viewer load (covers all per-page consumers) + cumulative-bytes caps in merge.js/img2pdf.js addFiles().
open_gaps.SEC-CSP-FRAME: INFO. frame-src 'self' blob: was added to the live CSP on 2026-06-13 for the Print tool (print.js). Allows only same-origin + same-origin-blob frames, no remote origins; frame-ancestors 'self' unchanged. Verified live this tick.

notes: 45 JS modules (+4 since the 2026-06-12 20:30 review: print.js TASK-352, nup.js TASK-353, margins.js TASK-354, booklet.js TASK-355). All four are security CLEAN and of the same one-shot imposition class: no new upload boundary (operate on the open viewer doc's own bytes via pdf.js doc.getData(), build a fresh pdf-lib doc, open doc never mutated, nothing leaves the browser); filenames sanitized [^a-zA-Z0-9._-]->_ slice(180) -> <base>_{margins,booklet,2up/4up}.pdf; status via textContent only (never innerHTML); safe Blob download + setTimeout URL.revokeObjectURL(1s); guards for no-doc/numPages==0 and window.PDFLib-unready; errors caught -> inline status + EventBus.ERROR; talk to the app only through EventBus (PDF_LOADED/PDF_CLEARED) + ActionRegistry. margins.js additionally validates the mm input (finite, 0..MAX_MM=100) before use; nup.js layout select strictly maps to {2,4}; booklet.js pads to a multiple of 4 with blank leaves (never an error). index.html additions are static markup (tool tabs + panels with number/select inputs, margins input min=0 max=100, aria-live status spans) — no user data interpolated, '&' correctly escaped in button labels. Deployment integrity: diff -rq repo web/ vs /var/www webroot is clean (served files match committed source). Full-JS audit across all 45 modules: every innerHTML hit is either a COMMENT or an 'innerHTML = ""' clear (no user/PDF content ever assigned to innerHTML); zero eval/Function/document.write/insertAdjacentHTML/outerHTML; zero fetch/XHR/importScripts/remote-src (100% client-side); no hardcoded secrets. upload.js still enforces ext+MIME+empty+size(50MB)+%PDF- magic with sanitized filenames. Live headers via curl: full resource CSP (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'; worker-src 'self' blob:; img-src 'self' blob: data:; connect-src 'self' blob:; frame-src 'self' blob:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; font-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'), X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff. nginx -t OK.
